mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, directly increasing present valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes remains below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward 20-day returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 5.0 percent signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in > 55% of months where UNRATE exceeds 5.0, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate broad-based consumer demand expansion, which boosts corporate revenues and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during an economic expansion reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses or Rank IC drops below 0.01 during expansion phases, or 5-day forward returns show no significant correlation with INDPRO growth.

mechanism_narrative: A consumer price index level above 220 indicates entrenched inflation that forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are positive in > 50% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 220, or the threshold signal yields a negative Sharpe ratio in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal predicts negative 60-day returns in > 55% of observations, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for monetary policy variables.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product reflects stronger aggregate economic activity that directly translates into higher corporate profits, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Rank IC for GDPC1 changes remains below 0.02 over a 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with quarterly GDP growth.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production alongside rising consumer prices signals a supply shock regime that squeezes profit margins and triggers risk-off sentiment, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in > 60% of stagflation regime observations, or the joint signal fails to achieve a positive information coefficient in walk-forward validation.
